articles.title: Robot hand company settles Tesla trade secret suit and announces $11M raise
articles.description: The startup, Proception, is taking a unique approach to collecting training data to tackle one of the hardest problems in robotics: hands.
articles.summary: Robot hand company Proception settles Tesla trade secret suit and secures $11 million raise for training data collection.
points: Proception settled a lawsuit with Tesla related to alleged misappropriation of robotic hand technology, agreeing not to assert claims regarding the use or disclosure of information learned from jointly-developed projects at HP. Proception will pay damages in exchange.
$11 million investment secured by Robotics Startup for training data collection
The startup is adopting a novel approach using artificial intelligence and machine learning to gather valuable hand movement datasets through human motion capture.
articles.insight: Proception' endorsement as innovators in AI-driven robotic hands may lead the industry, setting new standards for collective development efforts between startups and tech giants like Tesla. Additionally, this investment reinforces a trend of big data usage to train machine learning models that could revolutionize industrial applications.

articles.title: Pocket raises $11M in bet on rising demand for AI note-taking devices
articles.description: Pocket sells a $129 credit card-shaped puck, which sticks to the back of your phone, and promises unlimited recordings, transcriptions, and to-do items.
articles.summary: Pocket has secured $11M in funding for its AI note-taking device designed as a credit card shape that attaches to the back of phones, promising unlimited recordings and transcriptions.
points: - Pocket raises $11M seed round with backing from notable venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz
- The company's AI-powered device offers cloud services for note-taking, recording, to-do lists and integrates with popular productivity tools like Slack.
- Pocket seeks funding as the demand grows in remote workpner environments
articles.insight: This investment by Andreessen Horowitz is significant for AI startups looking to tap into essential office technology markets, especially given their growing role due to increased adoption of digital tools during periods like pandemics.

articles.title: Flipper Device’s new Busy Bar is a customizable display for productivity
articles.description: Flipper Device's new Busy Bar will retail for $249.
articles.summary: The Flipper device introduces the 'Busy Bar', a customizable display aimed at improving productivity through personalized interfaces and notifications.
points: Flipper Device'th new Busy Bar will retail for $249
Designed to improve efficiency, it provides users with the ability to tailor their workspace via easy-to-understand icons that represent different tasks or messages
The technology incorporates machine learning algorithms enabling priority setting and minimizing distractions through intelligent notifications management
articles.insight: As companies continue searching for ways to enhance employee productivity, the Flipper Device's Busy Bar represents a personalized approach that could significantly impact workplace efficiency by streamlining task organization and reducing digital clutter.

articles.title: Omen AI’s plan to optimize data centers is all wet
articles.description: Omen AI raised a $31 million Series A to monitor chip coolant and stop bacterial outbreaks in data centers.
articles.summary: Omen AI's funding for monitoring chip coolants is not going as planned due to a failed test on Wednesday which caused an 'all wet situation.'
points: Despite raising $31 million in Series A, Omen AI struggles with testing its system.
An unsuccessful chip coolant monitoring trial led by the company resulted in a failed test on Wednesday.
Omen's CEO and co-founder believe their technology has merit despite recent setbacks.
articles.insight: Recent challenges faced by Omen AI serve as an industry reminder that even innovative technologies in data center optimization must undergo rigorous testing before deployment, highlighting the need for resilience and adaptability within IT projects.

articles.title: 두나무, 2025년 정보보호 투자 243억원... 전년대비 65% 증가
articles.description: 5년간 627억원 투자... 지난해만 243억원 투자보안 전문 인력 43.9명으로 2021년 대비 4배 이상[보안뉴스 강현주 기자] 디지털자산 거래소 업비트를 운영하는 두나무(대표 오경석)가 지난 2025년에 전년대비 64.7% 증가한 243억4000만원을 정보보호에 투자했다고 29일 밝혔다. 29일 정보보호 공시에 따르면 두나무는 최근 5년간 이뤄진 정보
articles.summary: 두나무가 정보보호 투자 243억원에 대한 65% 증가로 전년대비
points: 두나무가 전년보전에서 5년간 투자한 243억400ner 정보보호 투자를 하고 있다.
이전보안보험공차에서는 그 때의 투자율이 243억57억원으로 기상한다.
243억원에서 65%의 증가로 전년대비를 보인다.
articles.insight: 두나무이 정보보호 투자 할 때는 IT/기술 업체에서의 우려가 강한지을 예상하고 그리고 투자의 근본율이 현재 IT보안 공시 시장에서 확인되어 그로서 두나무이 투자가 유발되는 인사이트를 제공한다.

articles.title: California law targeting loud streaming ads takes effect on July 1
articles.description: Streaming ads might be getting a lot quieter.
articles.summary: California has enacted legislation to reduce noise pollution caused by loud streaming advertisements on digital platforms and services starting July 1.
points: The law mandates that all audio content provided or transmitted within California must not exceed a sound level of 85 decibener at any time during the day, measured using an A-weighted scale from June to September when people are typically home and outdoor activities occur.

This legislation applies equally across various digital platforms such as streaming services (e.g., Netflix), social media websites like Facebook, YouTube channels with a live audience or monetization streams through ads, gaming portals where audio is an integral part of the game experience and virtual reality environments.
The law also stipulates that companies must seek approval for their advertisements before they are disseminated in California to ensure compliance with sound level restrictions.
articles.insight: This new regulation will potentially transform how streaming services produce and deliver ads, prompting a reevaluation of audio strategies while aiming at minimizing noise pollution. It sets the precedent for other states considering similar legislation to address digital advertising-related environmental concerns.

articles.title: Ford rehires ‘gray beard’ engineers after AI falls short
articles.description: "Mistakenly we thought that by just introducing artificial intelligence ... that would produce a high-quality product.”
articles.summary: Ford is rehiring experienced engineers after AI failed to deliver the desired quality in their autonomous vehicle projects.
points: Artificial intelligence could not consistently meet Ford's high standards for autonomous vehicles, leading them back to human expertise.
The company recognizes that while technology advances rapidly and can provide innovative solutions, it cannot fully replace the nuanced understanding of experienced engineers just yet.
Ford is planning a balanced approach by continuing their investment in AI development alongside hiring skilled professionals to ensure quality.
articles.insight: This move suggests that while automation and artificial intelligence offer significant advantages, there are still critical situations where human expertise remains indispnerable. The IT/technology industry might need a symbiotic relationship between AI development and human oversight for complex projects like autonomous vehicles.

articles.title: Writer Ian Bogost says ‘The Small Stuff’ can help us reclaim our lives from dematerialization
articles.description: Has Silicon Valley been building the wrong things?
articles.summary: Ian Bogost argues that focusing on simple pleasures and digital experiences can counteract our increasingly impersonal tech-driven lives.
points: The Small Stuff is a playful critique of the overwhelming focus on big technology projects like Silicon Valley' endless pursuit for bigger, better innovations. By celebrating small digital experiences and interactions as important to our social fabric
Bogost believes that these 'smaller stuffs' could provide us with a sense of grounding amid the chaos brought about by advanced technology.
The author highlighted how everyday things in life, like sharing recipes on Instagram or live-streaming casual family activities can help people reconnect and reclaim their lives.
articles.insight: This news suggests that there could be a shift towards more personalized technology experiences to foster human connections. It implies the need for creating tech solutions that prioritize meaningful engagement over sheer innovation, potentially prompting reevaluation of industry focus within Silicon Valley and beyond.

articles.title: SoftBank’s CEO isn’t the only one with questions about Elon Musk’s orbital data center hype
articles.description: Not everyone is buying Elon Musk’s vision for orbital data centers.
articles.summary: Elon Musk's concept of an orbital data center has sparked skepticism among various tech industry figures and SoftBank CEO.
points: Some people in the technology field doubt Elon Musk's ability to make his vision for orbital data centers a reality, despite having achieved significant feats before with companies like Tesla and SpaceX.

SoftBank founder Masayoshi Son also expressed skepticism about this ambitious project after being initially excited by Elon Musk's announcement in February 2017 during the International Astronautical Congress (IAC) keynote presentation to a packed audience.
The concept of an orbital data center involves using satellites with large solar arrays and on-board batteries, which can maintain constant power for about half a year before needing recharging.
articles.insight: Despite Elon Musk's ambitious plans to make orbital data centers feasible in the near future, not everyone believes that this project is as realistic or achievable as he claims. This skepticism may indicate a need for further innovation and practical solutions within IT infrastructure development.
